title: OpenObserve
description: Rust-based, petabyte-scale observability platform for logs, metrics, traces and real user monitoring; a lightweight Elasticsearch/Datadog alternative.
installation: This template runs the official single-node (local mode) image openobserve/openobserve on HTTP port 5080. DATASTORE: in local mode OpenObserve stores its metadata in an embedded SQLite DB and its ingested log/metric/trace data on local disk, so NO external database add-on is provisioned - everything lives on the mounted /data volume (ZO_DATA_DIR). For that reason the persistent volume MUST be retained across redeploys or all ingested data is lost; grow the volume to match your retention. BEFORE FIRST RUN you MUST rotate ZO_ROOT_USER_EMAIL and ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD - these create the initial admin account on first boot (password must be 8-128 chars with upper, lower, digit and special char). Log in at the root URL with those credentials to get your ingestion token. SCALE-OUT (NOT configured here): for HA/cluster mode OpenObserve instead needs an external PostgreSQL or MySQL metastore (ZO_META_STORE=postgres) plus S3-compatible object storage (ZO_LOCAL_MODE=false, ZO_LOCAL_MODE_STORAGE=s3 with ZO_S3_* settings) and the multi-role router/querier/ingester/compactor topology - point those at the postgres add-on and an external S3 bucket if you outgrow a single node. Telemetry is disabled via ZO_TELEMETRY=false.
